Unmuze v0.4.0 adds a new advanced format picker and a stronger release-readiness pipeline.

What's changed since v0.3.3:
- Added an advanced source format picker after URL inspection.
- Shows available source details where yt-dlp provides them: resolution, container, video codec, audio codec, bitrate, fps, and estimated file size.
- Keeps the simple Best and Balanced presets as the default path, so most users do not need to touch advanced options.
- Allows advanced users to choose a specific discovered source format for individual downloads.
- Polished the advanced format picker so Audio mode only shows audio-only source formats, while Video mode shows video and muxed formats.
- Shortened advanced format labels so values like resolution are not repeated in the dropdown.
- Improved the advanced format helper text so selected source formats and final output presets are easier to understand.
- Keeps playlist downloads on presets, because each playlist item can expose different formats.
- Improved keyboard and screen-reader support for tabs, selects, progress bars, switches, and history tables.
- Fixed late download progress events so completed, failed, or cancelled items do not get stale status updates.
- Hardened backend download tracking so duplicate IDs cannot orphan a running yt-dlp process.
- Improved managed-tool status checks so broken local tool files do not block fallback to working system tools.
- Added more robust URL parsing for supported platforms, including hosts with ports and userinfo.
- Added CI release-readiness checks for linting, frontend tests, production frontend build, Rust tests, version alignment, updater configuration, release tag alignment, and release notes.
- Release builds now stay as drafts until all platform assets are present, updater metadata is checked, and the version-specific notes file is applied.
- Updated README and BUILD docs for the new advanced format picker and release checks.

Installers and packages:
- Windows: use Unmuze_0.4.0_x64-setup.exe for the easiest install, or the MSI if you prefer Windows Installer packaging.
- macOS: use the DMG. The app.tar.gz and .sig files are used by the in-app updater.
- Linux: AppImage, DEB, and RPM packages are attached.

Notes:
- Unmuze still requires legally permitted, publicly accessible media. It does not bypass DRM, paywalls, login restrictions, private links, region locks, or other access controls.
- latest.json and .sig files are for signed in-app updates.
